GWAS with GAPIT

1.1_GWAS_with_GAPIT.Rmd

Before the gwaspr package can be used, we first need to run GWAS with GAPIT.

# Load our genotype file in hapmap format
myG <- read.csv("myG_hmp.csv", header = F)
# Load our phenotype file
myY <- read.csv("myY.csv")
# Load our covariate file
myCV <- read.csv("myCV.csv")

Genotype data (myG)

Our population, the LDP, was genotyped using an exome caputure array and filtered with the following criteria to yield a data set of 336,367 SNPs, stored in our object myG.

  • Only Bi-allelic = TRUE
  • Minimum Read Depth = 3
  • Minor Allele Frequency = >5%
  • Maximum Missing Frequency = 25%
  • Heterozygous = <25%
# Map + marker Info
myG[1:10,1:11]
# genotrype calls
myG[1:10,12:17]
DT::datatable(myG[1:20,1:20])

Phenotype data (myY) 

DT::datatable(myY)

Covariate data (myCV) 

DT::datatable(myCV)

Run GWAS

Run GWAS on the 3 traits myY

library(GAPIT)
# Run GWAS
myGAPIT <- GAPIT( Y = myY,
                  G = myG, 
                  PCA.total = 4,
                  model = c("GLM","MLM","MLMM","FarmCPU","BLINK"),
                  Random.model = F, Phenotype.View = F
                )

GWAS with Covariate

Run GWAS on the DTF_Sask_2017 trait with b as a covariate.

# Prep data
myY2 <- myY[,c("Name","DTF_Sask_2017")]
colnames(myY2)[2] <- "DTF_Sask_2017_b"
# Run GWAS with a covariate
myGAPIT <- GAPIT( Y = myY2,
                  G = myG, 
                  CV = myCV,
                  PCA.total = 0,
                  model = c("GLM","MLM","MLMM","FarmCPU","BLINK"),
                  Random.model = F, Phenotype.View = F
                )

On this page
